reasoning: The fertility rate in South Korea already increased to 0.75 in 2024, driven by a post-pandemic rebound in marriages. Marriages rose for the first time in 12 years in 2023 and rose even more sharply in 2024, setting the stage for 2025 births to remain elevated relative to the deeply depressed 2023 baseline of 0.72. Additionally, early indicators for 2025 (e.g. Q1 rates around 0.82) suggest the upward trend is holding. It is very likely that the total fertility rate for 2025 will exceed 0.72.
batch_queries: Will the fertility rate of South Korea increase from 2023 to 2025?
